verses.text: The gospel opens with 'the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham,' from Abraham onward. Rooted in promise. Christ comes as the heir of every covenant God made of old.
The gospel opens with 'the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham,' from Abraham onward. Rooted in promise. Christ comes as the heir of every covenant God made of old.
The gospel opens with 'the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham,' from Abraham onward. Rooted in promise. Christ comes as the heir of every covenant God made of old.
The line runs through Boaz of Ruth and on to David the king. Grace in the line. God wove even outsiders and sinners into the ancestry of His Son.
The line runs through Boaz of Ruth and on to David the king. Grace in the line. God wove even outsiders and sinners into the ancestry of His Son.
The line runs through Boaz of Ruth and on to David the king. Grace in the line. God wove even outsiders and sinners into the ancestry of His Son.
From Solomon the kings descend, down to the carrying away into Babylon. Through the throne. The royal line passes through both glory and downfall.
From Solomon the kings descend, down to the carrying away into Babylon. Through the throne. The royal line passes through both glory and downfall.

From Solomon the kings descend, down to the carrying away into Babylon. Through the throne. The royal line passes through both glory and downfall.
From Solomon the kings descend, down to the carrying away into Babylon. Through the throne. The royal line passes through both glory and downfall.
After the captivity the line continues to Joseph, husband of Mary, 'of whom was born Jesus.' Humbled at last. The royal house, brought low, brings forth the King of kings.

After the captivity the line continues to Joseph, husband of Mary, 'of whom was born Jesus.' Humbled at last. The royal house, brought low, brings forth the King of kings.
After the captivity the line continues to Joseph, husband of Mary, 'of whom was born Jesus.' Humbled at last. The royal house, brought low, brings forth the King of kings.
After the captivity the line continues to Joseph, husband of Mary, 'of whom was born Jesus.' Humbled at last. The royal house, brought low, brings forth the King of kings.
So the generations are reckoned in three sets of fourteen, from Abraham to Christ. Ordered history. God's plan unfolds by careful, measured stages.
Mary is found with child 'of the Holy Ghost'; Joseph, a just man, is minded to put her away quietly. Tender justice. He would spare her shame even while perplexed.
Mary is found with child 'of the Holy Ghost'; Joseph, a just man, is minded to put her away quietly. Tender justice. He would spare her shame even while perplexed.
An angel says, Fear not; 'thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins.' The Saviour's name. He came not merely to teach, but to save us from sin itself.
An angel says, Fear not; 'thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins.' The Saviour's name. He came not merely to teach, but to save us from sin itself.
This fulfils the prophecy: 'a virgin shall be with child... and they shall call his name Emmanuel... God with us.' The great wonder. In Christ, God Himself comes to dwell among men.
This fulfils the prophecy: 'a virgin shall be with child... and they shall call his name Emmanuel... God with us.' The great wonder. In Christ, God Himself comes to dwell among men.
Joseph obeys, takes Mary, and calls the child JESUS. Prompt obedience. He bows to God's word without delay or question.
